Timing: Acute/post-acute (2wk–2mo)
Key_caveat: General case series; ocular data on 19 survivors, no formal eye exam; N/exam/examiner in original table were wrong.

Timing: Long-term (>2y)
Key_caveat: SUDV, Gulu 2000; symptom survey, no eye exam.

Timing: Multiple timepoints
Key_caveat: 2007 Bundibugyo (BDBV); no eye exam (self-report symptoms). Original "Zaire/case series/N=103" was scrambled from Bwaka 1999 (its ref 4).

Timing: Long-term (>2y)
Key_caveat: Paediatric; 23 survivors/46 eyes; species not named in paper; original "Gulu 2000" note was mis-copied.

Timing: Survivor (>6m)
Key_caveat: SL 2013-16 paediatric survivors; self-report only. Species was wrongly BDBV; N 206→159; "Uganda 2007" note was a scrambled citation.

Timing: Survivor (>6m)
Key_caveat: Uveitis case series (128 uveitis patients); recruited 2022; examiner was ophthalmologist (original said non-specialist).

Timing: Long-term (>2y)
Key_caveat: Self-report survey, ZERO eye exams; original table listed slit-lamp/OCT/SUN in error.

Timing: Long-term (>2y)
Key_caveat: 521 survivors examined ~3.5y post-EVD; uveitis subtypes denominated among 69 uveitis cases.

Timing: Long-term (>2y)
Key_caveat: Guinea PostEboGui 802; ocular sequelae as composite category (non-specialist), no specific uveitis extractable.

Timing: At enrolment (median 350 days, IQR 223–491 after ETC discharge)
Key_caveat: Guinea PostEboGui; trained clinicians (not ophthalmologists); iridocyclitis as uveitis proxy.

Design: Longitudinal cohort study (secondary analysis of PREVAIL III; NCT02431923)
Timing: Baseline (median 317 days, IQR 271–366 from EVD onset) and 12-month follow-up visits
Key_caveat: PREVAIL III secondary analysis; 991 survivors (3679 counted all contacts); 889 had baseline eye exam.

Timing: Participants surveyed for PES every 6 months post-discharge; exact timing of blood/PBMC collection relative to EVD onset not stated
Key_caveat: Immunological study; PES defined by self-reported symptoms; no ocular exam.

Timing: 6-8 years post-recovery
Key_caveat: Self-report survey; 595 survivors (998 counted contacts); no clinical eye exam.

Design: Prospective interventional cohort study (protocol-driven EBOV RNA testing pre-cataract surgery)
Timing: Median 19 months (Phase 1) and 34 months (Phase 2) post-EVD diagnosis
Key_caveat: EVICT surgical-selection cohort (cataract surgery); not a prevalence sample.

Design: Prospective observational cohort study with 1-year follow-up (re-examination of previously studied symptomatic EVD survivors)
Timing: Multiple timepoints: baseline (~13.1 months post-discharge) and follow-up (~25.6 months post-discharge)
Key_caveat: 72 attended baseline, 57 analysed; symptomatic-referral cohort; species not named.

Design: Prospective cohort study; sub-analysis of EVICT study assessing cataract surgery outcomes and vision-related quality of life in EVD survivors
Timing: Not reported
Key_caveat: EVICT surgical-selection sub-analysis.

Design: Prospective cohort study using routinely collected MSF survivor clinic data
Timing: Post-EVD; mean 51.1 days (SD 41.2) from ETC discharge to first follow-up visit; clinic period February–June 2015
Examiner: Medical doctors at MSF clinic for initial VA assessment; ophthalmologists at Lowell and Ruth Gess UMC Eye Hospital for formal ocular diagnosis
Key_caveat: 166 enrolled, 97 referred/examined; species not named; RoB upgraded from Low.

Design: Retrospective multicenter cohort study; pediatric EVD triage/case-definition predictive score development in acute-phase EHU admissions; NOT an EVD ocular complications or survivor follow-up study
Timing: During acute EVD illness at Ebola holding unit admission (August 2014–March 2015); not survivor follow-up
Key_caveat: Paediatric acute-triage/case-definition study (N=1054 acute admissions); NOT an ocular/survivor study; conjunctivitis was a triage symptom only.

Design: Cross-sectional survey within a survivor cohort (mobile health clinic observational study)
Timing: MHC operated February 7, 2015 to June 10, 2016 (16 months); data collection initiated 9 months after outbreak began and ended 26 months later (7 months after Sierra Leone declared Ebola-free by WHO)
Examiner: Non-specialist (trained nurses and clinical officers; serious ocular cases referred to eye clinic)
Key_caveat: Mobile clinic, nurses (no slit-lamp); paper states uveitis frequency could not be determined.

Timing: Early (1-6m)
Key_caveat: Port Loko 277 cohort; early convalescence (~4m); examiner not stated.

Timing: median 109 days (IQR 91–210) from ETC discharge to first survivor clinic visit
Examiner: General clinician (non-ophthalmologist); specialist referral arranged for those with relevant symptoms
Key_caveat: Self-reported symptoms (7-day recall); no formal ophthalmic exam.

Timing: Long-term (›2y)
Key_caveat: Cataract-surgery cohort (25 survivors+12 controls); original N_Enrolled 277 was wrong.

Timing: March 2015 to March 2016 (EVICT study screening period); exact time from ETU discharge not stated
Key_caveat: EVICT selection (active uveitis / cataract-surgery candidates); corneal findings among affected eyes, not survivors.

Timing: Not stated in abstract; median ETU stay 21 +/- 18.4 days
Examiner: Ophthalmologist (comprehensive ophthalmic exam at Lowell and Ruth Gess Kissy Eye Hospital, Freetown)
Key_caveat: Conference abstract; same EVICT cohort as Fashina 2024 (S23); selection bias.

Timing: median 142 days (IQR 127–159) from ETU discharge to specimen collection
Key_caveat: EBOV RNA-persistence study; no ophthalmic exam, no ocular diagnoses.

Timing: Survivor (>6m)
Examiner: Ophthalmologist (eye investigator at ophthalmic departments of Donka National Hospital and Nzérékoré Regional Hospital; photographs reviewed by uveitis specialist)
Key_caveat: Guinea PostEboGui; uveitis subtypes among 46 uveitis cases; original "abstract of Fashina" note was wrong.

Design: Prospective observational case series of neurologic sequelae (neurologically-selected sub-cohort from a survivor clinic; ophthalmology performed as secondary assessment in specialist clinic subset only)
Timing: Study conducted February 4–May 10, 2016; preliminary clinic attendees seen median 430 days (IQR 401–473) after first positive diagnostic result
Key_caveat: Neurology-selected; ophthalmology in only 17/361; RoB upgraded from Low.

Timing: Recruited at ≥12 months post-discharge; median 18 months post-discharge at time of study (June 2016)
Key_caveat: Case-control; 27 survivors (81 counted controls); self-report disability questionnaire, no eye exam.

Timing: Acute phase during EVD treatment at ETC admission (June 2014 to April 2015); no post-discharge follow-up
Key_caveat: Acute paediatric admissions (2014-15); conjunctivitis 20/139 as admission symptom; no eye exam.

Timing: Survivor (>6m)
Key_caveat: PREVAIL III baseline; 754 counted controls; paediatric subgroup analysed.

Design: Retrospective review (conference abstract; ARVO Annual Meeting 2016)
Timing: Post-discharge; uveitis symptoms onset median 3 weeks (IQR 0.4-8.6 weeks; range 0-17.2 weeks) after discharge from Ebola treatment centre; time to ophthalmologic assessment not stated
Key_caveat: ARVO abstract; duplicate of S20 (Port Loko 277).

Timing: Baseline at median ~11 months post symptom onset; annual follow-up for up to 60 months (5 visits)
Key_caveat: PREVAIL longitudinal IOP study; uveitis listed only as IOP risk factor.

Timing: Survivor (>6m)
Key_caveat: ELWA 96 survivors; original "277 participants" note was mis-copied.

Timing: 2015 (post-acute EVD survivor clinic at ELWA Hospital)
Key_caveat: ELWA clinic methods paper; duplicate of S33 (96 survivors).

Timing: January 2016 to April 2017
Key_caveat: 14 eyes selected for the Ebola retinal lesion; not representative.

Timing: Survivor (>6m; ~13.5m)
Examiner: Local ophthalmic clinical officers (first 35% of anterior chamber exams supervised by UK ophthalmologist); retinal images graded by 2 independent masked UK ophthalmologists with specialist interest in medical retina; third consultant as arbitrator
Key_caveat: Case-control, 82 survivors; symptomatic-referral; original timing (1-6m) was wrong.

Timing: Baseline mean 408 days post-ETU discharge; quarterly visits over approximately 3 years
Key_caveat: Symptom questionnaire, no ophthalmologist.

Design: Post-hoc retrospective cross-sectional chart review (sub-analysis of EVICT study cohort)
Timing: Post-hoc analysis of EVICT study charts; timing relative to acute EVD not specified in abstract
Examiner: Not specified (medical chart review of EVICT study data)
Key_caveat: Conference abstract of the Steptoe 2017 EVICT case-control; examiner not stated.

Timing: Campaign March 2015 to March 2016; retrospective programmatic data
Key_caveat: Programmatic cohort, 2667; mixed ophthalmologist/mid-level examiners.

Timing: March 7 to April 24, 2015 (baseline examination)
Key_caveat: Port Loko 277 (overlaps S20); uveitis reported per eye.

Timing: January 2015 to March 2017; visits ranged from 0 to 30 months post-ETU discharge, with 60% at 6–18 months and 30% at ≥24 months
Examiner: Non-specialist (ELWA clinic clinicians; slit-lamp examination dependent on availability of trained clinicians)
Key_caveat: General survivor clinic chart review; slit-lamp use variable.

Design: Baseline cross-sectional analysis within a 5-year prospective longitudinal cohort study (PREVAIL III eye substudy)
Timing: Post-acute; median 338 days (IQR 293–385) from symptom onset to enrollment; mean additional 100 days (SD 94) from enrollment to eye examination
Key_caveat: PREVAIL III eye substudy (564 survivors); overlaps S54.

Design: Prospective case series; secondary analysis of EVICT study cataract surgery cohort with 12-month follow-up and histopathology
Timing: Post-acute; patients identified March 2015–March 2016; screened June 2016–August 2017; surgical outcomes reported at 6 and 12 months post-MSICS
Key_caveat: EVICT cataract-surgery case series (34 operated); surgical selection.

Design: Program implementation report with descriptive cross-sectional ophthalmic screening
Timing: Post-acute; mean 4.6 months ± 1.8 SD (range: 24 days to 8.5 months) from EVD symptom onset to evaluation
Examiner: Ophthalmologist (visiting international and trained DRC national ophthalmologists)
Key_caveat: DRC 2018-2020 programme screening; original "Liberia 2014-2016" note was mis-copied.

Timing: Long-term (>2y)
Key_caveat: DRC 2018-20 case-control (120 survivors); independent of EVICT/PREVAIL.

Timing: Multiple timepoints
Key_caveat: Published Muwonge 2025 report (BMC Medicine) of the Uganda-2022 SUDV cohort; no eye exam. Duplicate preprint ("Severe long-term", S47) removed from corpus. DRC "2/237" data once attributed here belonged to Mwanza 2024 (S44).

Timing: Long-term (>2y)
Examiner: Trained study nurses and medical officer
Key_caveat: Bundibugyo (BDBV) 2007–08 cohort at ~16–17y; 40 survivors; self-report symptoms only (vision problems 22.5%), no eye exam. Original "SUDV"/"24 months"/"2022" were wrong; same outbreak as S03 Clark.

Timing: Convalescence (Phase 1: June 2016; Phase 2: July-August 2017)
Key_caveat: EVICT; enrolled for severe ocular disease; uveitis 92% = lifetime history, not prevalence.

Design: Cross-sectional (post-hoc secondary analysis of EVICT study baseline data)
Key_caveat: Sierra Leone EVICT-screening (125 pts/250 eyes); original "BDBV/Uganda/16y" note was mis-copied.

Timing: Median 898 days (range 622–1309) after ETU discharge or acute EVD; specimens collected 2017–2018
Key_caveat: All-male semen-persistence study; ocular findings secondary (tonometry performed).

Timing: Baseline evaluation (timing from EVD not stated in abstract)
Key_caveat: ARVO abstract; PREVAIL III cohort (789); overlaps S42/S54.

Design: Prospective longitudinal cohort study with EVD survivors and close-contact controls
Timing: Median 358 days (IQR 313–405) after symptom onset for baseline visit
